assertions.title: Recreated PR 2923 scope remains auth-upload priority propagation only

steps.expected: Only auth_files.go (MODIFIED) and auth_files_upload_test.go (ADDED) in PR file list
steps.observed: Files: auth_files.go (29 additions, 14 deletions, MODIFIED), auth_files_upload_test.go (69 additions, 0 deletions, ADDED)

steps.expected: Only auth_files.go and auth_files_upload_test.go touched in local commit
steps.observed: auth_files.go (43 changes, 14 deletions), auth_files_upload_test.go (317 additions). Exactly 2 files touched.

evidence.note: Both upstream PR and local commit touch only auth_files.go and auth_files_upload_test.go. Local commit has additional test coverage (317 lines vs 69 in upstream) but scope is confined to the same two files.

assertions.title: Uploaded top-level priority becomes a runtime auth attribute immediately

steps.expected: Test passes, confirming uploaded OAuth JSON with top-level priority becomes runtime attribute immediately

assertions.title: Both supported upload entry points produce the same synthesized runtime auth state

steps.expected: Test passes, confirming multipart and raw body uploads produce equivalent runtime state

frictions.description: Windows PowerShell 5.1 does not support && operator for command chaining; required using ; instead
frictions.resolution: Used semicolon (;) separator instead of && for cd and go test commands

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. VAL-PR2923-001 confirms scope is limited to auth_files.go and auth_files_upload_test.go (2 files only). VAL-PR2923-002 confirms uploaded priority propagates immediately to runtime auth. VAL-PR2923-003 confirms multipart and raw body uploads produce equivalent runtime state.
